raw_cv_text:
Felix Vance
/Bitmapp3r
Education
Imperial College London London, United Kingdom
MEng in Computing Sep 2023 – Jun 2027
St. Olave’s Grammar School Orpington, United Kingdom
GCSEs: Six Grade 9s, One Grade 8, Three Grade 7s Sep 2015 – Jun 2022
A-Levels: Computer Science, Mathematics, Further Mathematics, Physics | A*s in All Subjects
Experience
Full-Stack App Developer | My Salah Mat Jun 2025 – Present
• Developing a cross-platform app mobile app that will be pitched to investors using Flutter for the frontend and
Firebase as the backend
• Responsible for deciding the application architecture and technology stack
• Implementing a feature to allow users to save posts in a chronological structure
Projects
QuickMed | Flutter, Dart Jul 2025
• As part of a hackathon, developed a medical mobile app to allow patients to quickly transfer data to and from an
NFC tag and a phone; designed with humanitarian crises in mind
• Implemented loading and storing of medical JSON data
Readventure | NextJS, React, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, Firebase, GitLab CI/CD May 2025 – Jun 2025
• Created a webapp to improve children’s interest in reading by providing stories with choices that affect the
narrative and ending
• Implemented a genre filtering system to the story collection to help users find what they would like to read
WACC Compiler | Scala, Parsley, x86-64 Assembly, GitLab CI/CD Jan 2025 – Mar 2025
• Implemented a compiler for the WACC programming language which involved lexical, syntactic, and semantic
analysis, along with x86-64 code generation
• Extended the compiler by implementing peephole optimisations to the generated assembly code
PintOS Operating System | C, GDB Oct 2025 – Dec 2025
• implemented core operating system functionality and features to the ‘PintOS’ operating system such as CPU
scheduling, threading, support for user programs, and memory management
• Implemented the advanced BSD scheduler as an alternative to the default CPU scheduler
Aarch64 ARMv8 Emulator, Assembler and Wireless Morse Code Device | C Jan 2025 – Mar 2025
• Developed an emulator and assembler for the Aarch64 mode of the ARMv8 ISA that was then used to create an
assembly program to flash an LED connected to the Raspberry Pi’s GPIO pins
• Created a wireless Morse code device using the Raspberry Pi’s GPIO pins that can send and receive messages
wirelessly to and from another device
Technical Skills
Languages: Java, Python, C/C++, SQL, JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML/CSS, Scala, Dart, Haskell, Assembly
(x86-64, ARMv8)
Frameworks: Nextjs, React, Flutter
Developer Tools: Git, Firebase, VS Code, Visual Studio, PyCharm, IntelliJ, Android Studio
Libraries: Parsley, Matplotlib
Volunteering and Other Experience
Teacher Assistant | ‘Life Changing’ Tuition Aug 2023
• Volunteered at ‘Life Changing Tuition’ to help primary school students prepare for 11+ exams
• Helped mark exam papers, set up the venue, and also worked alongside Teachers to help students’ confusion over
certain topics
